Tokyo (Shibuya).name: Tiger Gyoza Kaikan Udagawacho
Tokyo (Shibuya).description: Spacious seating options compared to most tiny Shibuya shops. Great casual atmosphere for groups sharing giant plates of gyoza.
Tokyo (Shibuya).table_for_9_likelihood: High (If booking an early dinner slot)
Tokyo (Shibuya).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins (Highly recommended to book ahead for 9)

Tokyo (Shibuya).name: Nagi Shokudo
Tokyo (Shibuya).description: An enduring Shibuya staple serving incredible, hearty vegan set meals. Cozy, so advance communication for a group of 9 is necessary.
Tokyo (Shibuya).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Requires booking the main back-table space)
Tokyo (Shibuya).approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-45 mins (Walk-in for 9 is very tough without booking)

Tokyo (Shibuya).name: Shibuya Joumon
Tokyo (Shibuya).description: Incredibly lively Izakaya specializing in skewered meats and vegetables. High energy and perfect for a group celebrating.
Tokyo (Shibuya).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (They have larger sunken kotatsu tables; must book weeks out)
Tokyo (Shibuya).approx_wait_time_walkin: 45-60+ mins (Rarely accommodates a walk-in of 9 in prime hours)

Tokyo (Shibuya).name: Sushi Shin
Tokyo (Shibuya).description: An exquisite, high-end Edomae sushi experience. Elegant, quiet, and handles premium seasonal fish with mastery.
Tokyo (Shibuya).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Counter focus; group of 9 requires a private room buyout or whole-counter booking)

Tokyo (Shibuya).name: Ichiran Shibuya
Tokyo (Shibuya).description: The quintessential solo-booth tonkotsu ramen experience. Fast, iconic, and reliably customizable via ticket machines.
Tokyo (Shibuya).table_for_9_likelihood: High (But you will be split up across individual solo booths)
Tokyo (Shibuya).approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-60 mins (Queue moves quickly, but getting 9 continuous booths takes extra time)

Tokyo (Shibuya).name: BiNDi Shibuya
Tokyo (Shibuya).description: Tucked away from the main scramble, this low-key bistro serves masterful small plates paired beautifully with local sake.
Tokyo (Shibuya).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Small interior, booking the back group section is required)
Tokyo (Shibuya).approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-40 mins (If table space allows; booking highly advised)

Tokyo (Shinjuku).name: Tsunahachi Shinjuku Souhonten
Tokyo (Shinjuku).description: Operating since 1924, this multi-floor historic restaurant makes serving incredible, light tempura to large families look easy.
Tokyo (Shinjuku).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Multi-floor layout accommodates large groups well with reservations)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-45 mins during peak hours if unreserved

Tokyo (Shinjuku).name: Ain Soph. Journey Shinjuku
Tokyo (Shinjuku).description: Famous for their heavenly vegan matcha pancakes and rich multi-course plant-based lunch sets. Very foreigner-friendly.
Tokyo (Shinjuku).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Spanning several intimate floors; booking ahead is mandatory)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-40 mins (Highly chaotic walk-in for big numbers; reserve online)

Tokyo (Shinjuku).name: Imafuku Sukiyaki
Tokyo (Shinjuku).description: Meltingly tender A5 Wagyu beef cooked tableside in a rich, sweet soy broth. A masterclass in traditional Tokyo beef cuisine.
Tokyo (Shinjuku).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (They have exquisite private rooms that perfectly fit 8-10 people)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Only (Must book private rooms 3-4 weeks out)

Tokyo (Shinjuku).name: New York Grill (Park Hyatt)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).description: Perched on the 52nd floor with breathtaking floor-to-ceiling city views. World-class wine list and exceptional service.
Tokyo (Shinjuku).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Spacious grand dining room, easily handles large tables via early booking)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Strongly Advised (Bar area walk-ins have 30-60 min waits; no large group tables guaranteed)

Tokyo (Shinjuku).name: Omoide Yokocho Asadachi
Tokyo (Shinjuku).description: A throwback to Showa-era Tokyo along Memory Lane. Grilling savory skewers over charcoal in a legendary narrow alley.
Tokyo (Shinjuku).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Alley stalls are tiny; you will likely need to split into 3 groups of 3)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-45 mins (Constant line rotation, expect immediate group splitting)

Tokyo (Shinjuku).name: Sake Bar Yoramu
Tokyo (Shinjuku).description: An incredible curation of rare, unpasteurized, and aged sakes poured by an incredibly knowledgeable expert host.
Tokyo (Shinjuku).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Very intimate setting; require a private group reservation layout upfront)
Tokyo (Shinjuku).approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Only (Tiny footprint can't support sudden large walk-ins)

Tokyo (Asakusa).name: Asakusa Imahan
Tokyo (Asakusa).description: Founded in 1895. Incredible tatami rooms where kimono-clad staff prepare legendary sukiyaki right before your eyes.
Tokyo (Asakusa).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Large historic building with ample private dining rooms available)
Tokyo (Asakusa).approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-60 mins at lunch if unbooked; dinner requires reservations for large rooms

Tokyo (Asakusa).name: Sukunahikona Veggie
Tokyo (Asakusa).description: Delightful plant-based takes on classic comforting noodle dishes right near the temple grounds.
Tokyo (Asakusa).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Cozy interior; try to visit outside peak lunch windows)
Tokyo (Asakusa).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins during peak weekend daytime traffic

Tokyo (Asakusa).name: Asakusa Monjayaki Yuraku
Tokyo (Asakusa).description: The true downtown Tokyo comfort food experience. A runny, savory, intensely flavorful pancake grilled directly on a tabletop iron plate.
Tokyo (Asakusa).table_for_9_likelihood: High (They can cluster tables together, but everyone cooks in sub-groups of 3-4 per grill)
Tokyo (Asakusa).approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-40 mins in evening blocks; easy rotation if arriving early (before 6 PM)

Tokyo (Asakusa).name: Hommage
Tokyo (Asakusa).description: Two Michelin stars. Exceptional French culinary techniques utilizing highly local, seasonal Japanese ingredients.
Tokyo (Asakusa).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Highly exclusive dining room; requires months of planning)

Tokyo (Asakusa).name: Asakusa Menchi
Tokyo (Asakusa).description: Incredibly juicy, crispy, deep-fried panko-crusted beef cutlets. Ideal for a quick mid-walk street food treat.
Tokyo (Asakusa).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Counter service window; buy and enjoy in the adjacent designated eating space)
Tokyo (Asakusa).approx_wait_time_walkin: 5-15 mins in line (Fast window service, no seated table delays)

Tokyo (Asakusa).name: Asakusa Mugitoro Honten
Tokyo (Asakusa).description: Overlooking the Sumida River, this historic spot specializes in comforting tororo poured over barley rice.
Tokyo (Asakusa).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Spacious multi-story layout with beautiful views)
Tokyo (Asakusa).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins for the buffet floor; easier access on weekday lunch slots

Osaka (Kita / Umeda).name: Epitaphe Beer & Grill
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).description: Spacious modern layout inside the grand Umeda commercial sectors, providing easy open seating configurations for big parties.
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Modern open floor-plan makes grouping easy)
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).approx_wait_time_walkin: 10-25 mins (Spacious building cushions peak lunch wait timelines)

Osaka (Kita / Umeda).name: Green Earth Osaka
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).description: One of Osaka's oldest dedicated vegetarian establishments. Serves stellar plant-based burgers, wraps, and daily curry sets.
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Call ahead so they can join their central modular tables together)
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins; highly recommended to drop a notice call 1 hour before arrival

Osaka (Kita / Umeda).name: Kushikatsu Daruma Umeda Chayamachi
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).description: The quintessential Osaka street-food experience—clean, crisp skewered meats and vegetables dipped in rich savory sauce.
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).table_for_9_likelihood: High (The Umeda branch relies heavily on booth tables which comfortably seat larger family groups)
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-40 mins (Line cycles fast; booths fit large groups well)

Osaka (Kita / Umeda).name: Kashiwaya Osaka
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).description: Three Michelin stars. Breathtakingly quiet and beautiful traditional sukiya-style house setting serving spectacular masterwork courses.
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Requires extensive outreach to secure their signature large private group tatami halls)

Osaka (Kita / Umeda).name: Ramen Honolu Umeda
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).description: Incredibly rich, comforting chicken paitan broth ramen that is highly accessible and deeply flavorful.
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-35 mins (High rotation turnover speed helps keep queues low)

Osaka (Kita / Umeda).name: Teppanyaki Oumi
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).description: Tucked cleanly away inside the InterContinental, this refined teppan counter offers an intimate look at premium sizzled meats.
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (They can coordinate private side-by-side grill counters if booked in advance)
Osaka (Kita / Umeda).approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Strongly Recommended (Walk-in seating capacity for 9 is very rare)

Osaka (Minami / Namba).name: Chibo Sennichimae Honten
Osaka (Minami / Namba).description: A massive, multi-floor landmark flagship location. The theater of having your okonomiyaki flipped on the table grills is universally loved.
Osaka (Minami / Namba).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Multi-level seating allows groups to easily sit together at adjacent table grills)
Osaka (Minami / Namba).approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-45 mins (Large size manages crowd lines efficiently)

Osaka (Minami / Namba).name: Gluten-Free & Vegan OKONOMIYAKI Vim
Osaka (Minami / Namba).description: A modern revelation in Namba. Recreates the classic, fluffy texture of Osaka savory street food completely plant-based.
Osaka (Minami / Namba).table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Advance reservations are necessary due to its unique, highly sought-after menu)
Osaka (Minami / Namba).approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-50 mins if walking in during peak evening dinner shifts

Osaka (Minami / Namba).name: Mizuno Dotonbori
Osaka (Minami / Namba).description: Operating since 1945 along the glowing canal paths. Legendary for its 'Yamaimo-yaki' using 100% grated mountain yam dough instead of flour.
Osaka (Minami / Namba).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Massive queues outside; walk-ins for 9 are incredibly tough unless arriving at 10:45 AM)
Osaka (Minami / Namba).approx_wait_time_walkin: 45-90 mins (Intense Dotonbori foot traffic makes lines slow for large groups)

Osaka (Minami / Namba).name: Matsusakagyu Yakiniku M Juichibancho
Osaka (Minami / Namba).description: Specializes in whole-cow purchasing of legendary Matsusaka beef. You grill assorted premium cuts to perfection over smoky tabletop grills.
Osaka (Minami / Namba).table_for_9_likelihood: High (They feature lovely wood-paneled private rooms that fit large parties beautifully)
Osaka (Minami / Namba).approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-60 mins for walk-ins; reserve online weeks early to lock down a large private booth room

Osaka (Minami / Namba).name: Ganso Kushikatsu Daruma Shinsekai Sohonten
Osaka (Minami / Namba).description: The absolute birthplace of Kushikatsu under the shadow of Tsutenkaku Tower. Crispy skewers dipped in their famous single-dip secret sauce.
Osaka (Minami / Namba).table_for_9_likelihood: Low (The historic original branch features only 12 counter seats. Better to aim for their massive nearby 'Janjan' or 'Dotombori' branches)
Osaka (Minami / Namba).approx_wait_time_walkin: 40-75 mins at original counter; larger branches nearby process lines in 20-30 mins

Osaka (Minami / Namba).name: Dotonbori Kamukura Sennichimae
Osaka (Minami / Namba).description: Features a uniquely sweet, savory clear soup loaded with crunchy napa cabbage and tender pork slices. A distinct local favorite.
Osaka (Minami / Namba).table_for_9_likelihood: High (Large, long continuous counter arrays that handle high-volume turnover quickly)
Osaka (Minami / Namba).approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins (Fast queue clearing speed keeps things moving smoothly)

Kyoto.name: Ganko Takasegawa Nijoen
Kyoto.description: Housed inside a stunning 400-year-old historic mansion overlooking a traditional stroll garden and private stream. Exceptional value and perfect for families.
Kyoto.table_for_9_likelihood: High (Huge historic layout containing numerous spacious private group dining rooms)
Kyoto.approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-40 mins if unbooked, but reservation slots open months early online

Kyoto.name: Shigetsu (Tenryu-ji Temple)
Kyoto.description: Located deep within the beautiful grounds of Arashiyama's Tenryu-ji Temple. Pure zen-like atmosphere serving entirely plant-based seasonal dishes.
Kyoto.table_for_9_likelihood: High (Large, expansive tatami temple halls designed for big visiting groups; reservation mandatory)
Kyoto.approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Only (Must be booked via temple portal in advance)

Kyoto.name: Kyoto Gion Otagi
Kyoto.description: A breathtaking showcase of Kyoto's subtle, vegetable-and-dashi focused high culinary arts, capturing seasonal change beautifully.
Kyoto.table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Highly exclusive; reservation tracking via concierge services is essential)
Kyoto.approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Only (No walk-in seats map onto large parties)

Kyoto.name: Kikunoi Honten
Kyoto.description: World-famous 3 Michelin-starred landmark culinary estate tucked alongside the quiet green Higashiyama hills.
Kyoto.table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Requires months of direct notice and custom allocation confirmation for group spaces)
Kyoto.approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Only (Requires matching concierge booking protocols)

Kyoto.name: Chao Chao Gyoza Sanjo Kawaramachi
Kyoto.description: Famous for their incredibly crispy, tiny 'winged' gyoza served on hot iron pans. Fun, frantic, and incredibly cheap.
Kyoto.table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (They have a back table tier, but expect a lively wait together outside during peak dinner times)
Kyoto.approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-60 mins (One of the most packed tourist casual queues in downtown Kyoto)

Kyoto.name: Monk Kyoto
Kyoto.description: Set beautifully near the Philosopher’s Path, featuring a brilliant wood-fired multi-course menu utilizing fresh hyper-local farm vegetables.
Kyoto.table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Extremely limited total table counts; requires checking their web booking engine right as slots open)

Hiroshima.name: Okonomimura (Multi-stall building)
Hiroshima.description: A legendary 3-story theme building housing over 20 independent okonomiyaki stalls. Incredible sensory overload that kids love.
Hiroshima.table_for_9_likelihood: High (If individual counters are full, you can easily split 5 and 4 across adjacent vendors)
Hiroshima.approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins (You can drift across floors to find stall openings for your sub-groups)

Hiroshima.name: Nagataya Vegetarian Choice
Hiroshima.description: Right by the Peace Park, offering an exceptional, dedicated plant-based menu using custom vegan savory sauce and soy meat alternatives.
Hiroshima.table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Incredibly popular tourist spot; lines move quickly but large groups face wait splits)
Hiroshima.approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-55 mins during standard evening lines; queue forms early down the street corner

Hiroshima.name: Kakigoya (The Oyster Hut) Ujina
Hiroshima.description: A casual, open-air coastal warehouse setup where you grab baskets of giant, plump local oysters and roast them yourself over tabletop pits.
Hiroshima.table_for_9_likelihood: High (Huge, open warehouse picnic table setting that handles big groups effortlessly)
Hiroshima.approx_wait_time_walkin: 10-25 mins (Spacious building footprint absorbs crowds well on weekends)

Hiroshima.name: Hiroshima Kaiseki Futaba
Hiroshima.description: Refined private rooms showcasing spectacular local seafood caught fresh from the Seto Inland Sea.
Hiroshima.table_for_9_likelihood: High (They specialize in private multi-course banquets for travelling family groups)
Hiroshima.approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Advised (Schedules private dining layouts 2-3 weeks out)

Hiroshima.name: Bakudan-ya Honten
Hiroshima.description: Famous local style cold noodles served with piles of fresh cabbage and scallions, dipped in a customizable fiery chili-sesame broth.
Hiroshima.approx_wait_time_walkin: 15-30 mins; table shifts are relatively fast

Hiroshima.name: Anago Meshi Ueno (Miyajima Guchi)
Hiroshima.description: Operating since 1901 right near the Miyajima ferry landing. Masterfully grilled wild conger eel brushed with a deeply savory century-old tare sauce.
Hiroshima.table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Massive historic cult following; expect major queues unless you grab their exceptional take-out bento boxes)
Hiroshima.approx_wait_time_walkin: 45-90+ mins (Extremely dense queue footprint; buying their hot bento boxes to eat on the coast takes 5 mins)

Hakone.name: Hakone Akatsukian
Hakone.description: Beautiful traditional mountain-style lodge house famous for pristine buckwheat noodles ground using pure Hakone mineral spring water.
Hakone.table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Spacious rustic interior, but lunch hours get incredibly busy with tour traffic)
Hakone.approx_wait_time_walkin: 30-50 mins at peak lunch windows; arriving by 11:15 AM cuts wait lines significantly

Hakone.name: Hakone Karaaku
Hakone.cuisine_type: Organic Tofu & Local Mountain Vegetable Cuisine
Hakone.description: Charming mountain stop highlighting gorgeous local tofu varieties alongside wild gathered seasonal highland vegetables.
Hakone.table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (Quaint layout, reach out early to secure group table bookings)
Hakone.approx_wait_time_walkin: 20-40 mins if unreserved; phone checking ahead is highly recommended

Hakone.name: Tamura Ginkatsu-tei
Hakone.cuisine_type: Tofu Katsu Ni (Deep-fried tofu stuffed with minced pork)
Hakone.description: Hakone's absolute signature dish. Fluffy local tofu stuffed with seasoned savory minced meat, deep-fried panko-style, then simmered in dashi and egg.
Hakone.table_for_9_likelihood: Low (Legendary queues; group walk-ins during lunch are brutal without using their ticket wait system early)
Hakone.approx_wait_time_walkin: 45-90 mins (Uses a digital ticketing machine system at the entrance porch layout)

Hakone.name: Hakone Itoh Dining by Nobu
Hakone.description: A stunning architectural collaboration with Chef Nobu Matsuhisa, serving premium local cuts of grilled black Wagyu alongside mountain vistas.
Hakone.table_for_9_likelihood: Medium (They have gorgeous group seating blocks, but advance evening bookings are essential)
Hakone.approx_wait_time_walkin: Reservation Strongly Advised (Walk-ins rarely secure large dining blocks)

Hakone.name: Bakery & Table Hakone
Hakone.description: Perched perfectly right along the shores of Lake Ashi. Grab incredible fresh curried bread buns and eat at their outdoor foot-bath terrace counter.
Hakone.table_for_9_likelihood: High (Large upstairs multi-tier cafe floors handle high volume gracefully)
Hakone.approx_wait_time_walkin: 10-25 mins for bakery checkout lines; patio tables rotate frequently

Hakone.name: Hakone Chaya
Hakone.description: A quiet, wonderfully unassuming rest house oasis away from the heavy train crowds, serving comforting mountain sets.
Hakone.approx_wait_time_walkin: 10-20 mins (Wonderfully calm compared to lakefront properties)
